prompt: Write a Python script that conducts a positive selection analysis on influenza A H3N2 hemagglutinin to identify sites evolving under positive selection for vaccine strain selection research. The script should: (1) download 1,000 H3N2 HA sequences from NCBI Influenza Virus Resource (2010–2024), (2) align with MAFFT and construct a codon-aware MSA, (3) infer a maximum-likelihood tree with IQ-TREE (GTR+G+I), (4) run PAML codeml M7 vs M8 site model comparison to detect positively selected sites (BEB > 0.95), (5) map positively selected sites onto HA trimer structure (PDB 3LZG) and annotate proximity to receptor-binding site and known antibody epitopes, (6) output: annotated MSA, PAML results CSV, and PyMOL session file with positively selected sites highlighted. Include PAML codeml input control file generation.
source_citation: Derived from NCBI Influenza Virus Resource and RCSB PDB (3LZG); target: influenza A H3N2 HA; organism: Influenza A virus; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: orthomyxovirus surface antigen adaptive evolution analysis
